body_txt: The villain is at the hero's mercy but taunts him for being too cowardly to take his life and the hero's response is along the lines of: "It's not mine to take", as he sheathes his weapon (or hands it to another character). The motive is usually The Only One Allowed to Defeat You (because the speaker recognizes they are not the "Only One"), though Anti Heroes may be prone to Throw 'Em to the Wolves instead. Expect this to be said during a Sword over Head moment. Compare/contrast Sparing Them the Dirty Work when a character (usually one of the (anti) heroes) kills another (usually the villain) in order to prevent another (often more heroic character) from having to "dirty" their hands with murder.
